prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(initiate_campaign_entity upstream_line_1)
(reserve_equipment_for_campaign upstream_line_1 equipment_reactor_1)
(assign_operator_to_campaign upstream_line_1 operator_1)
(confirm_operator_handover upstream_line_1 operator_1)
(mark_upstream_stage_ready upstream_line_1 stage_fermentation operator_1)
(assign_support_team_to_campaign upstream_line_1 support_team_alpha)
(complete_upstream_setup_with_support upstream_line_1 stage_fermentation support_team_alpha)
(release_support_team_from_campaign upstream_line_1 support_team_alpha)
(release_operator_from_campaign upstream_line_1 operator_1)
(initiate_campaign_entity downstream_line_1)
(reserve_equipment_for_campaign downstream_line_1 equipment_filler_1)
(assign_operator_to_campaign downstream_line_1 operator_1)
(confirm_operator_handover downstream_line_1 operator_1)
(mark_downstream_stage_ready downstream_line_1 stage_purification operator_1)
(assign_support_team_to_campaign downstream_line_1 support_team_alpha)
(complete_downstream_setup_with_support downstream_line_1 stage_purification support_team_alpha)
(release_support_team_from_campaign downstream_line_1 support_team_alpha)
(release_operator_from_campaign downstream_line_1 operator_1)
(assemble_batch_record_document upstream_line_1 downstream_line_1 stage_fermentation stage_purification batch_record_br001)
(assign_operator_to_campaign upstream_line_1 operator_1)
(activate_batch_record_document_for_qc batch_record_br001 upstream_line_1 operator_1)
(release_operator_from_campaign upstream_line_1 operator_1)
(release_upstream_line upstream_line_1 batch_record_br001)
(release_downstream_line downstream_line_1 batch_record_br001)
(initiate_campaign_entity manufacturing_order_mo1)
(reserve_equipment_for_campaign manufacturing_order_mo1 equipment_packer_1)
(assign_operator_to_campaign manufacturing_order_mo1 operator_1)
(confirm_operator_handover manufacturing_order_mo1 operator_1)
(initiate_regulatory_inspection_path manufacturing_order_mo1 inspection_kit_1 operator_1)
(assign_support_team_to_campaign manufacturing_order_mo1 support_team_alpha)
(complete_regulatory_inspection_setup manufacturing_order_mo1 support_team_alpha)
(attach_certificate_to_order manufacturing_order_mo1 certificate_1)
(attach_certificate_for_inspection manufacturing_order_mo1 certificate_1)
(complete_regulatory_inspection_and_mark_release manufacturing_order_mo1)
(bind_release_document_by_operator manufacturing_order_mo1 release_doc_1 operator_1)
(release_order_and_return_equipment manufacturing_order_mo1 equipment_packer_1 release_doc_1)
(release_operator_from_campaign manufacturing_order_mo1 operator_1)
(release_support_team_from_campaign manufacturing_order_mo1 support_team_alpha)
(detach_certificate_from_order manufacturing_order_mo1 certificate_1)
(initiate_campaign_entity manufacturing_order_mo2)
(reserve_equipment_for_campaign manufacturing_order_mo2 equipment_packer_1)
(assign_operator_to_campaign manufacturing_order_mo2 operator_1)
(confirm_operator_handover manufacturing_order_mo2 operator_1)
(issue_qc_sample_for_order manufacturing_order_mo2 qc_sample_2 batch_record_br001)
(start_qc_test_sequence manufacturing_order_mo2 qc_sample_2 batch_record_br001 operator_1)
(attach_component_kit_to_order manufacturing_order_mo2 component_kit_1)
(assign_support_team_to_campaign manufacturing_order_mo2 support_team_alpha)
(execute_inspection_sequence_phase1 manufacturing_order_mo2 component_kit_1 support_team_alpha qc_sample_2 batch_record_br001)
(attach_certificate_to_order manufacturing_order_mo2 certificate_1)
(initiate_final_inspection_phase manufacturing_order_mo2 certificate_1 qc_sample_2 batch_record_br001)
(finalize_order_release manufacturing_order_mo2)
(bind_release_document_by_operator manufacturing_order_mo2 release_doc_1 operator_1)
(release_order_and_return_equipment manufacturing_order_mo2 equipment_packer_1 release_doc_1)
(release_operator_from_campaign manufacturing_order_mo2 operator_1)
(release_support_team_from_campaign manufacturing_order_mo2 support_team_alpha)
(assign_operator_to_campaign upstream_line_1 operator_1)
(bind_release_document_by_operator upstream_line_1 release_doc_1 operator_1)
(release_line_and_return_equipment upstream_line_1 equipment_reactor_1 release_doc_1)
(release_operator_from_campaign upstream_line_1 operator_1)
(assign_operator_to_campaign downstream_line_1 operator_1)
(bind_release_document_by_operator downstream_line_1 release_doc_1 operator_1)
(release_downstream_line_and_return_equipment downstream_line_1 equipment_filler_1 release_doc_1)
(release_operator_from_campaign downstream_line_1 operator_1)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem involves a manufacturing campaign with upstream_line_1, downstream_line_1, and two manufacturing orders: mo1 and mo2. Both orders are assigned to both lines, and linked to the same batch_record_br001, different qc_samples, etc.


## Investigating Manufacturing Setup  
- The problem centers on a manufacturing campaign involving upstream_line_1 and downstream_line_1.  
- Two manufacturing orders, mo1 and mo2, are assigned to both lines and linked to batch_record_br001.  
- Each order connects to different qc_samples, suggesting distinct quality control processes.

## Investigating Manufacturing Setup  
- The goal is to release all entities: upstream_line_1, downstream_line_1, mo1, and mo2.  
- No campaign entities are initialized yet, so start by initiating all: upstream_line_1, downstream_line_1, manufacturing_order_mo1, and manufacturing_order_mo2.  

## Planning Equipment Allocation  
- Each campaign entity (lines and orders) requires equipment allocation; available equipment includes reactor_1, filler_1, and packer_1.  
- Assign reactor_1 to upstream_line_1 and filler_1 to downstream_line_1; packer_1 must be shared between mo1 and mo2, but equipment is unit-specific and reserved.  

## Exploring Release Process  
- Releasing entities likely requires processing lines first, as batch record assembly needs both lines ready and stages completed.  
- Different release paths exist, such as release_upstream_line or release_downstream_line, to achieve campaign_entity_ready_for_release.
Continuing from the existing summary, here's the flow of new insights:

## Exploring Release Process  
- Orders mo1 and mo2 follow regulatory inspection paths using inspection kits; mo1 uses inspection_kit_1 (available), but mo2’s inspection_kit_2 is unavailable, suggesting mo2 needs an alternate path.  
- Releasing lines requires campaign_entity_ready_for_release, dependent on batch_record_document_qc_activated, which needs batch_record active, upstream_line ready, and operator assigned.  

## Planning Batch Record Assembly  
- Assembling batch_record requires both lines ready, with variants like standard (both stages and segments ready/completed) or commit-based (material committed, segments not completed).  
- For example, complete_upstream_setup_with_support makes upstream_line_ready and segment_completed, needing prior handover, support, and stage readiness.

## Exploring Release Process  
- Operator assignment and support team allocation are crucial for marking stages ready, starting with upstream_line_1 using operator_1 and support_team_alpha.  
- After completing upstream setup, support and operator can be released, making them available for downstream_line_1.  

## Planning Operator and Support Management  
- Downstream_line_1 follows a similar process, requiring operator_1 reassignment after release and support_team_alpha reassignment post-upstream.  
- Both lines need stages marked ready and setups completed to achieve line_ready and segment_completed statuses.  

## Executing Batch Record Assembly  
- After both lines are ready, assemble_batch_record_document links stages and activates the batch record, requiring prior readiness checks.  
- Operator reassignment to upstream_line_1 is needed temporarily to activate QC for the batch record, then released again.  

## Finalizing Line Release  
- With QC activated, release_upstream_line and similar processes can mark lines as ready_for_release, completing the release process.  
- The workflow ensures all prerequisites, like batch activation and segment completion, are met before final release.

## Initiating Order Processing  
- Manufacturing orders like mo1 require equipment reservation, starting with packer_1 since it's the only available option.  
- Operator assignment and handover confirmation are needed, ensuring mo1 is ready for subsequent steps like QC sampling.  

## Managing Inspection Phases  
- Inspection sequences, such as phase1 or phase2, depend on QC starting, component kit linkage, and support assignment, with phase1 not requiring downstream approval.  
- Final inspection phases or result compilation vary based on upstream and downstream approvals, with variants covering different approval combinations.  

## Finalizing Order Release  
- Finalizing order release involves attaching packaging after inspection approval and requires operator assignment, segment completion, and line readiness.  
- Complete_inspection_and_mark_release sets the order as released and ready_for_release, but campaign_entity_released requires additional steps like binding release documents and returning equipment.  

## Handling Equipment and Resources  
- Orders must be processed sequentially due to limited resources like one packer_1 and one release document, requiring full release before moving to the next order.  
- Releasing equipment happens after completing actions like release_order_and_return_equipment, ensuring resources are available for subsequent orders.

## Continuing Order Processing  
- After releasing mo1, equipment packer_1 and release_doc_1 become available again, allowing processing of mo2.  
- Operator_1 remains assigned to mo1 post-release, requiring release_operator_from_campaign to free them for mo2, alongside releasing support_team_alpha.  

## Handling Component Kit Limitations  
- Mo2 cannot proceed with inspection sequences like mo1 due to component_kit_1 being linked to mo1 and no additional kits available.  
- Execute_inspection_sequence_phase1 requires order_linked_to_component_kit, creating a bottleneck for mo2's inspection process.  

## Exploring Alternative Paths  
- Regulatory review paths, like process_qc_with_regulatory_review, require batch_upstream_approved, which isn't available, limiting this option for mo2.  
- Without a component kit, mo2 can't achieve qc_inspection_ready, essential for final inspection, suggesting a need for additional resources or workflow adjustments.

## Evaluating Order Paths  
- Mo1 can use the regulatory inspection path with inspection_kit_1, avoiding the need for a component kit.  
- Mo2 requires the QC path, needing component_kit_1 and batch QC activation for inspection readiness.  

## Managing Limited Certificates  
- Both paths require linking certificate_1, but only one is available, creating a bottleneck.  
- Detach_certificate_from_order allows reusing the certificate after releasing an order, enabling sequential processing.  

## Optimizing Resource Allocation  
- Complete one order fully, including release and equipment return, before detaching resources like certificates for the next order.  
- Operator and support team management, along with equipment like packer_1, must be coordinated to ensure availability for subsequent orders.

## Managing Line Processing  
- Upstream_line_1 requires operator_1 assignment for binding release documents, followed by releasing equipment and documents.  
- After releasing upstream_line_1, operator_1 is freed, allowing assignment to downstream_line_1 for similar binding and release actions.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
